---
title: "4.11 2D Array Creation and Access"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## 2D Array Creation and Access

### Two-dimensional arrays model data in rows and columns

The textbook introduces 2D arrays as the natural structure for grid-like information.

![Rows and columns as lockers](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/2DLockers.jpg){fig-align="center" width="74%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- rows run horizontally
- columns run vertically
- 2D arrays are useful for tables, seats, boards, and pictures
:::

## Array Storage

### Java stores a 2D array as an array of arrays

![Row-major storage idea](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/rowMajor.png){fig-align="center" width="68%"}

![Rows and columns as nested arrays](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/ArrayRowsAndCols.png){fig-align="center" width="72%"}

Retained concepts:

::: {.tight-list}
- 2D arrays are arrays whose elements are themselves arrays
- AP questions assume <span class="term">row-major order</span>
- the outer array represents rows and the inner arrays represent columns
:::

## Declaring and Creating 2D Arrays

### Syntax extends the 1D array pattern

```java
int[][] ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
String[][] seatingChart = new String[3][2];
```

Key points kept from the textbook:

::: {.tight-list}
- `datatype[][] name` declares a 2D array variable
- `new` creates the rows and columns
- elements receive default values
- the total element count is rows times columns
:::

## Initializer Lists

### Each row is itself a 1D initializer list

```java
int[][] arr2D = { {1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6} };
```

Students should connect this to nested 1D arrays rather than treating it as a completely new syntax system.

## Set and Get Values

### Use `[row][col]` with row first and column second

Retained access rule:

::: {.tight-list}
- the first index selects the row
- the second index selects the column
- assignment changes one specific cell
- retrieval reads one specific cell
:::

This row-first convention is central for AP CSA tracing.

## Row and Column Length

### The outer array length gives rows; a row length gives columns

```java
arr.length
arr[0].length
```

Retained rules:

::: {.tight-list}
- `arr.length` is the number of rows
- `arr[0].length` is the number of columns in one row
- row indices and column indices both start at `0`
- invalid coordinates cause `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- identify row-major organization in a 2D structure
- declare and create 2D arrays with `new`
- build 2D arrays with initializer lists
- access and modify values with `[row][col]`
- use `length` correctly for rows and columns
- <span class="term">4.11.7 Coding Challenge: ASCII Art</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that a 2D array is an array of arrays
- identify row-major order as the AP assumption
- write the syntax for declaring and creating a 2D array
- explain the meaning of `[row][col]`
- distinguish `arr.length` from `arr[0].length`
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
